summary:
The new RNTCP guidelines require persisting symptoms such as a cough lasting over two weeks; an HIV-positive individual by itself may not be considered suspicious enough for active TB according to this standard. Thus, among the choices presented, only one correctly reflects this requirement accurately while excluding those lacking specified symptomatology. 

Option B fits best since other conditions typically include explicit reference to prolonged respiratory issues characteristic of potential TB cases.

Thus, among the choices presented, only one correctly reflects this requirement accurately while excluding those lacking specified symptomatology. 

Option B fits best since other conditions typically include explicit reference to prolonged respiratory issues characteristic of potential TB cases.. Answer choice: B
